2603.25138.title: Reinforcement learning for quantum processes with memory
2603.25138.paper_summary: In reinforcement learning, an agent interacts sequentially with an environment to maximize a reward, receiving only partial, probabilistic feedback. This creates a fundamental exploration-exploitation trade-off: the agent must explore to learn the hidden dynamics while exploiting this knowledge to maximize its target objective. While extensively studied classically, applying this framework to quantum systems requires dealing with hidden quantum states that evolve via unknown dynamics. We formalize this problem via a framework where the environment maintains a hidden quantum memory evolving via unknown quantum channels, and the agent intervenes sequentially using quantum instruments. For this setting, we adapt an optimistic maximum-likelihood estimation algorithm. We extend the analysis to continuous action spaces, allowing us to model general positive operator-valued measures (POVMs). By controlling the propagation of estimation errors through quantum channels and instruments, we prove that the cumulative regret of our strategy scales as $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(\sqrt{K})$ over $K$ episodes. Furthermore, via a reduction to the multi-armed quantum bandit problem, we establish information-theoretic lower bounds demonstrating that this sublinear scaling is strictly optimal up to polylogarithmic factors. As a physical application, we consider state-agnostic work extraction. When extracting free energy from a sequence of non-i.i.d. quantum states correlated by a hidden memory, any lack of knowledge about the source leads to thermodynamic dissipation. In our setting, the mathematical regret exactly quantifies this cumulative dissipation. Using our adaptive algorithm, the agent uses past energy outcomes to improve its extraction protocol on the fly, achieving sublinear cumulative dissipation, and, consequently, an asymptotically zero dissipation rate.
2603.25138.paper_summary_zh: 在強化學習中，代理人與環境進行順序互動以最大化獎勵，僅獲得部分的、概率性的反饋。這創造了一個基本的探索與利用的權衡：代理人必須探索以學習隱藏的動態，同時利用這些知識來最大化其目標。雖然這一框架在傳統上已被廣泛研究，但將其應用於量子系統需要處理通過未知動態演變的隱藏量子狀態。我們通過一個框架來形式化這個問題，其中環境維持一個通過未知量子通道演變的隱藏量子記憶，而代理人則使用量子工具進行順序干預。對於這種設定，我們調整了一個樂觀的最大似然估計算法。我們將分析擴展到連續動作空間，讓我們能夠建模一般的正算子值測度（POVMs）。通過控制估計誤差在量子通道和工具中的傳播，我們證明了我們策略的累積後悔在 $K$ 次回合中按 $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(\sqrt{K})$ 的比例增長。此外，通過將其簡化為多臂量子強盜問題，我們建立了信息論的下界，證明這一亞線性增長在多對數因子範圍內是嚴格最優的。作為一個物理應用，我們考慮狀態無關的工作提取。在從一系列由隱藏記憶相關的非獨立同分佈量子狀態中提取自由能時，對於來源的任何缺乏知識都會導致熱力學耗散。在我們的設定中，數學後悔精確量化了這一累積耗散。使用我們的自適應算法，代理人利用過去的能量結果即時改進其提取協議，實現亞線性的累積耗散，並因此達到漸近的零耗散率。
2603.25138.author: Josep Lumbreras et.al.
2603.25138.authors: Josep Lumbreras, Ruo Cheng Huang, Yanglin Hu, Marco Fanizza, Mile Gu
